mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or 20-day forward returns during rate declines are statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal's out-of-sample accuracy drops below 45% in a walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal income and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer demand and construction activity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint decline periods yield positive 20-day returns in > 60% of cases, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample regression.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production above its 12-month moving average reflects strengthening supply-side capacity and corporate output growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power during recovery phases, with 5-day forward returns showing no significant positive skew (t-stat < 1.5) or Rank IC < 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects expanding aggregate demand and corporate revenue fundamentals, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth periods fail to produce positive 60-day returns in > 50% of observations, or rolling 2-year Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above a 0.4% monthly threshold forces expectations of tighter monetary policy and higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in > 65% of high-inflation trigger months, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample classification tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening and wage growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Concurrent payroll gains and unemployment declines yield negative 20-day returns in > 55% of cases, or the interaction effect fails to improve model fit over additive terms.

mechanism_narrative: Real personal income growth that fails to outpace consumer price inflation erodes household purchasing power and suppresses corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of instances where income growth lags inflation, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below chance in walk-forward validation.
